It is therefore,\nrecommended that suitable provision should be made to the effect that\nfrom the share to be distributed to :1 daughter on intestacy, the amount\nor value of the property so provided by the father during his lifetime\nshould be deducted. provided the following conditions in favour of\nthe daughter are ful\ufb01lled :--\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_828\">(i) the niaking of such provision is evidenced in writing. whether or\nnot the \\vritin\u00ab__~ is stamped or registered: and\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_829\">(ii) the amount of the provision or its value. on each individual occa-\n</blockquote><p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1031\">sion, is not less than \ufb01ve hundred rupees.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1032\">&#x27;Paragraph 5.88.\n</p><p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1033\">&#x27;-&#x27;Paragraph 6. I0.\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_830\">&quot;Paragraph 8. 12.\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_831\">&#x27;Paragraph 8. l2.\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_832\">5Pnragraph 8.12.\n</blockquote>\n\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_101\">277</span>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_833\">Intcslates other than Parsis: <a href=\"/doc/219409/\" id=\"a_907\">Sections 31</a> to 49\n\n(31) Where the intestate governed by section 33 leaves no surviving issue,\nparent, brother or sister of the whole blood or issue of such brother or sister,\nhiswhole estate should pass to the widow On this point the provision in section\n46, Intestates Estates Act, 1952 (Eng.) 4) worth adopting, being in consonance\nwith the present day sentiments. Section 33 should be amended accordingly.&#x27;\n\n(32) Section 33A, providing for the compulsory share of the widow where\nthe deceased has left no lineal descendants, should be amended so as to--\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_834\">(i) increase the amount from 5,000 rupees to Rs. 35,000; and\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_835\">(ii) increase the rate of interest, from 4 p.c. to 9 p.c.*\n\n(33) Section 34 provides that where the intestate has left no widow, his\nproperty shall go-\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_836\">(a) to his lineal descendants; or\n\n</blockquote><blockquote id=\"blockquote_837\">(b) to those who are of kindred to him, not being lineal descendants, accord-\n</blockquote><p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1034\">ing to the prescribed rules; or\n\n</p><p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1035\">(c) if no kindred are left, then to the Government.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1036\">The most usual cases under the last part are of illegitimate persons. Although,\ntechnically, their property goes to Government, it appears that there was practice\nin the past of the Crown (now, the State) re-granting the property to certain\nrelatives, in case of illegitimate persons.&#x27;\n\nThe Law Commission has not been able to ascertain whether these arrange-\nments still continue. However, social justice requires that the need for such\narrangements should be considered in the context of section 34.&#x27;\n\n(34) There is also a point of drafting pertaining to section 34. The corres-\nponding provision in the <a href=\"/doc/685111/\" id=\"a_908\">Hindu Succession Act</a> is more precise than the provi-\nsion inthe <a href=\"/doc/677520/\" id=\"a_909\">section 34</a> of Indian Succession Act. <a href=\"/doc/685111/\" id=\"a_910\">The Hindu Succession Act</a> (sec-\ntion 29) provides that the Government takes the property subject to all the obli-\ngations of the heir. It is recommended that in section 34, for the last six words\n&quot;it shall . - . . . . . . .. the Government&quot;, the words &quot;such property shall devolve\non the Government; and the Government shall take the property subject to all\nthe obligations and libabilities to which an heir would have been subject,&quot; should\n\nbe substituted.&#x27;\n\n(35) Even in cases governed by sections 37--39, there should be succes-\nsion per stripes, as that would be more in consonance with the general sense of\nthe community. Section 37 should be revised as under, to carry out this object.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1037\">Revised section 37\n\n&quot;37. .\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1068\">(59) Section 69 should be revised as under :--\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_857\">&quot;69. In illustration (vii), the words &quot;since it\nwould be against public policy&quot; should be added at the end, And, in illustration\n</p><p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_1077\">(viii), the words &quot;since there is evidence of contrary intention&quot; could be added\n\nat the end for the purpose.&quot;\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1078\">(68) Section 100 should be suitably re-drafted so as to refer to a reputation\nor relationship, but not necessarily a reputation of legitimacy. The object could\nbe achieved by substituting, in place of the words &quot;being such relative&quot;, the\nwords &quot;being a child, son or daughter ortotherwise standing in the relationship\n\nin question&quot;.\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1079\">Revised section 100 would then&#x27; read thus---\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_869\">&quot;l00. In the absence of any intimation to the contrary in a Will, the word\n&quot;child&quot;, the words &quot;son&quot;, the word &quot;daughter&quot;, or any word which expresses\n\n&quot;relationship, is to be understood as denoting only a legitimate relative, or,\nwhere there is no such legitimate relative, a person who has acquired, at\nthe date of the will, the reputation of being a child, son or daughter or\notherwise standing in the relationship in question.&quot;\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1080\">(This recommendation to be carried out, only if the alternative recommenda-\ntion to revise the section is not accepted).&#x27;\n\n(69) Section 100, illustration (Vii), is anachronistic. Both from the juristic\npoint of view and on wider considerations of social justice, a bequest to an illegiti-\nmate child, whether already begotten or otherwise, should not be regarded as\nagainst public policy. <a href=\"/doc/515323/\" id=\"a_913\">In the Transfer of Property Act, 1882</a> there is no provision\ncorresponding &quot;to section 100, illustration (vii), as regards gifts during life time\n</p><p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1081\">-aiid&#x27;there&#x27; is no reason for retaining any such provision in the law of testamentary\nsuccessioiil&#x27;\n\n(70) Illegitimate children should be regarded as prima facie included with-\ninithe scope of reationship, in the absence of expression of a contrary intention.\nIf a person begets an illegitimate child, it would not, in general, be unrealistic\nto presume that he would, in making a testamentary disposition, like to bene\ufb01t\nhis illegitimate children as well. There will still remain scope for the expres-\nsion of acontrary intention by him. But, subject to this safeguard, it would be\nproper, as a matter of social justice, to reverse the present rule, which was\nframed at a time when the notions of society on the subject under consideration\nwere much more rigid than they are now. Section 100 should be revised and\nnew section 100A should be inserted for the purpose, as recommended in the\n\n. Report?\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1082\">(71) In regard to section 105, illustration (vi), dealing with the situa-\ntion of _&quot;co&#x27;mmorients&quot; (deaths of two or more persons in a common disaster),\nattention is drawn to the Law Commission&#x27;s recommendation in its Report on\ntlxie.<a href=\"/doc/1953529/\" id=\"a_914\">Evidence Act</a>.&#x27;\n\nIf the  does not, in any manner, indicate an intention that the two persons\nare to take it jointly, the presumption should be in favour of a tenancy in common.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1083\">&#x27;Paragraphs 16.45,&#x27; The amendment should de\ufb01ne &#x27;personal injury&#x27; as (i)\nincluding any disease and any impairment of a person&#x27;s physical or mental condition,\nand (ii) excluding injury caused by malicious prosecution.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1138\">For the purpose, an appropriate Explanation could be added to  306.&#x27;\n(125) Section 309 should be modi\ufb01ed on the following lines 2\n\n(1) Whether or not the_ will provides for the payment of remuneration to\nan executor or administrator, so long as the will does not prohibit such\npayment, an executor or administrator shall be entitled to receive or\nretain\u00bb reasonable commission or agency charges.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1139\">(2) Unless the will otherwise provides or the court otherwise permits, those\ncharges shall not be a higher rate than that for the time being \ufb01xed\nin respect of the Administrator General by or under the <a href=\"/doc/163971107/\" id=\"a_915\">Administrator-\nGeneral&#x27;s Act, 1963</a>.&#x27;\n\n(126) From section 310, the case where purchase is sanctioned by the court\nshould be excluded.&#x27; &#x27;\n\n(127) Section .311 speaks of a &quot;direction to the contrary&quot;, but does not\nspecify where the direction is &quot;to be contained.&#x27; It is recommended that in section\n311, it should be made clear that the direction may be either in the will or in\nthe probate.&#x27;\n\n(128) Section 317 should be amended, by addingthe following Exception\nto the section&#x27; : .\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_967\">&quot;Excepn&#x27;on---Where the Adminilstrator-General is the executor or adminis-\ntrator, he shall not be bound to exhibit an inventory or an account of the\nestate under this sub-section, unless the court has directed him to do so,\nin which case he shall exhibit the inventory or the account, as the case may\nbe, within such period as the Court may allow; but copies of the accounts\nmaintained by him shall be filed in court.&quot; -\n</blockquote>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_968\">(129) Section 324 (2), and the illustrationito the section, should be deleted.&#x27;\n\n(130) If the provisions of the <a href=\"/doc/685111/\" id=\"a_916\">Succession Act</a> relating to domicile are extended\nto A Hindus etc. (as recommended separately), sub-section (3) of section 324\nwill also have to be deleted, as a Consequential change.&#x27;\n\n(131) In section 330, the illustration should be revised, so as to express the\namounts in decimal coinage.&#x27; The section\nshould be amended by inserting an Explanation on the above lines.&#x27;\n\nAlso, in illustration (i) to the section, the words &quot;Imperial Bank of &#x27;India&quot;\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_969\">should be replaced by the words &quot;State Bank of India.&quot;\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1148\">(133) Section 362 needs a Verbal change, namely, the last four words should\nread &quot;wasting on the part of the executor&quot; (and not &quot;wasting of the executor&quot;).\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_1149\">(134) In section 367, a new sub-section should be inserted, as under :-~-\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1150\">&#x27;&#x27;(2) Where there has been a grant of administration in ct country other\nthan the country of domicile and other than India, the executor or adminis-\u00bb\nrator may, with the permission of the Court, take the some action as he\ncould have taken if there had been a grant in a country of domicile other\nthan India; and the provisions-of this section shall, with necessary modi-\n\ufb01cations, apply to the case as they apply if there had been a grant in a\ncountry of--domicfle Other than India.&quot;\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1151\">Succession Certi\ufb01cates--Sections 370 to 390\n\n(135) Section 370 bans the grant of succession ceri\ufb01cate, inter alia, in cases\nwhere letters of administration ( or probate) are mandatory&#x27;. Letters of adminis-\ntration are mandatory in the case of Christians other than Indian Christians\ndying intestate----to mention the most usual situation. The restriction in section\n370 is not required, except where probate is mandatory. The bar in section 370\nshould be limited only to cases where probate is mandatory, In the case of\nprobate, the will has to be proved and it is understandable that without proof\nof the will, payment should not be made on the strength of :1 mere succession\ncerti\ufb01cate. But this reasoning does not apply with the same force to letters of\nadministration, where title is sought to be derived on the basis of a right of\ninheritance conferred by law and no formal writing is, in general, to be proved.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_1152\">Accordingly, section 370(1) should be revised&#x27; as under :--\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_970\">&quot;(1) A succession certi\ufb01cate (hereinafter in this Part referred to as a\ncerti\ufb01cate) shall not be granted under the Part with respect to any debt\nor security to which a right is required by section 212 or section 213 to be\nestablished by . . . . . . . . .. probate.&quot;\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_1153\">(Existing proviso to be&#x27; omitted, as a consequential change).\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1154\">(136) There should also be inserted in section 370, a provision for the\ngrant of succession certi\ufb01cate on survivorship. By making this amendment, the\nlegislature would be merely giving recognition to what is already the practice in\nsome part of India. Although a certi\ufb01cate, issued to certify survivorship, should\nbe, strictly speaking, described as a &quot;Survivorship certi\ufb01cate&quot;, it is not proposed\nto change the present nomenclature (&quot;Succession Certi\ufb01cate&quot;), since it has,\nby now, become familiar to all concerned.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_1155\">Accordingly, the following proviso should be added to section 370 (1) :\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_971\">&quot;Provided that nothing contained in this section shall be deemed to prevent\nthe grant of a certi\ufb01cate to any person claiming to be entitled on survivorship\nto the effects of a deceased Hindu, Mohammedan, Buddhist, Sikh, Iain or\nParsi or to any part thereof, in respect of any debt or security by &#x27;reason\nthat the right thereto is claimed by survmorshzp and not by succession&#x27;. . 3\n\n(137) Section 371 should be revised as follows 1--\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1156\">&quot;371. The District Judge within whose jurisdiction the deceased, at the\ntime of his death, had a fixed place of abode, or any property, movable or\nimmovable, may grant a certi\ufb01cate under this Part&#x27;.&#x27;&#x27;\n\n1Paragraph 42. 3.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1157\">&#x27;Paragraph 42 . 2, footnote.\n</p><p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1158\">&#x27;Paragraph 46.8.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1159\">&#x27;Section 212(1).\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1160\">&#x27;Paragraphs 48.4 and 48.5.\n</p><p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1161\">&#x27;Paragraphs 48.6 and 48 . 8.\n</p><p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1162\">&#x27;Paragraphs 48.13 and 48.17.\n</p>\n\n\n<span class=\"hidden_text\" id=\"span_115\">291</span>\n\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1163\">(138) To section 372, an Explanation should be inserted below :-\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_972\">&quot;Explanation---Where the applicant for succession certi\ufb01cate is a minor, he\nmay apply through a next friend as if he were a plaintiff in a suit, and the\nprovisions <a href=\"/doc/161831507/\" id=\"a_917\">of the Code of Civil Procedure</a>, 1908, shall, so far as may be,\napply in relation to such next friend as they apply in relation to a next\nfriend sueing under Code&#x27;.&quot;\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Precedent\" id=\"p_1164\">(139) In section 379, a new sub-section should be inserted as follows&#x27; 1--\n</p>\n<blockquote id=\"blockquote_973\">&quot;(4) If the application is allowed with or without condition, the sum shall\nnot be refunded, even if, by reason of breach of the condition, the\ncerti\ufb01cate cannot be granted.&quot;\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1165\">(140) It is also recommended that the provisions of sub-section (3) of section\n379 should be made subject to those of new sub-section (4), which is proposed\nto be inserted, as above, in section 379.\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1166\">Section 379 (3) should, therefore, begin as follows :~--\n</p><blockquote id=\"blockquote_974\">&quot;(3) Subject to the provisions of sub-section (4) any sum received - - . - --3&quot;.\n</blockquote>\n<p data-structure=\"Section\" id=\"p_1167\">(141) New section 386A should be inserted, as recommended, to provide\nthe facility of succession certi\ufb01cate to properties other than debts&#x27;\n\nSuccession by homicide (proposed section 390-A)\n\n(142) A new section 390A should be inserted, disqualifying a person who\ncommits culpable homicide, or abets its commission, from inheriting, or from\ntaking as a legatee,----\n</p>\n<p data-structure=\"Conclusion\" id=\"p_1168\">(a) the property of the person whose death is so caused;
